components.title: GRC Pipeline
components.description: A six-week compliance-as-code pipeline: Terraform infrastructure enforcing NIST 800-53 controls, Rego/OPA policies that evaluate the plan, a GitHub Actions gate that blocks non-compliant merges, and Cosign keyless signing that produces a verifiable, tamper-evident chain of custody for the evidence.
components.purpose: Enforce and prove NIST 800-53 control implementation on AWS S3 infrastructure, and produce signed, verifiable evidence of that enforcement.

control-implementations.description: Controls implemented by the GRC Pipeline's Terraform baseline (week 1), enforced by the Rego policy gate (weeks 2-3), and proven with a signed evidence bundle (week 4).

implemented-requirements.description: Server-side encryption (AES-256) is enabled by default on both the primary and log S3 buckets via aws_s3_bucket_server_side_encryption_configuration, so data at rest is protected without relying on a person to configure it correctly. The Rego policy sc28_encryption_aws.rego denies any aws_s3_bucket that lacks a matching encryption configuration, gated on every pull request.

links.text: Signed evidence bundle (week 4 chain of custody, verified CHAIN INTACT)

implemented-requirements.remarks: The .sha256 sidecar and .sig.bundle signature file sit alongside this bundle at the same path in week-4/evidence/. Run week-4/verify-evidence.sh against all three to confirm CHAIN INTACT.

implemented-requirements.description: aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block enforces all four public-access flags (block_public_acls, block_public_policy, ignore_public_acls, restrict_public_buckets) as true on every bucket, preventing any accidental public exposure. The Rego policy ac3_no_public_aws.rego denies any bucket where the block is missing or any flag is not true.

links.text: Signed evidence bundle (week 4 chain of custody, verified CHAIN INTACT)

implemented-requirements.remarks: The .sha256 sidecar and .sig.bundle signature file sit alongside this bundle at the same path in week-4/evidence/. Run week-4/verify-evidence.sh against all three to confirm CHAIN INTACT.

implemented-requirements.description: The primary bucket has aws_s3_bucket_logging enabled, delivering access logs to a dedicated log bucket (aws_s3_bucket_acl set to log-delivery-write via aws_s3_bucket_ownership_controls), so every access to the primary bucket produces an audit record containing who accessed what and when.

links.text: Signed evidence bundle (week 4 chain of custody, verified CHAIN INTACT)

implemented-requirements.remarks: The .sha256 sidecar and .sig.bundle signature file sit alongside this bundle at the same path in week-4/evidence/. Run week-4/verify-evidence.sh against all three to confirm CHAIN INTACT.

implemented-requirements.description: The provider's default_tags block applies four required tags (Project, Environment, ManagedBy, ComplianceScope) to every taggable resource automatically, so configuration settings are consistent and traceable without relying on a person to tag resources by hand. The Rego policy cm6_required_tags_aws.rego denies any taggable resource missing one of the four tags.

links.text: Signed evidence bundle (week 4 chain of custody, verified CHAIN INTACT)

implemented-requirements.remarks: The .sha256 sidecar and .sig.bundle signature file sit alongside this bundle at the same path in week-4/evidence/. Run week-4/verify-evidence.sh against all three to confirm CHAIN INTACT.
